Title: Phosphatidylcholine:diacylglycerol cholinephosphotransferase’s unique regulation of castor bean oil quality

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL1 promoter (constitutive/galactose-inducible in yeast expression system)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: triricinoleoyl triacylglycerol (3-OH-TAG); ricinoleate-enriched seed oil TAG
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial seed oil rich in ricinoleic acid
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ER acyl-CoA-dependent TAG assembly, ricinoleate-enrichment branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: acylates diacylglycerol with acyl-CoA to form triacylglycerol, strongly favoring ricinoleoyl-containing DAG species
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for ricinoleate enrichment in TAG
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly expressed during castor seed development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested in yeast microsomes; active in developing castor endosperm microsomal context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strongly promotes synthesis of ricinoleate-rich TAG and virtually excludes acylation of nonhydroxylated DAG in the presence of ricinoleoyl-containing DAG
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcDGAT2 showed highest activity with 2-OH-DAG plus ricinoleoyl-CoA, supporting production of 3-OH-TAG. 2. RcDGAT2 preferred ricinoleoyl-containing DAG over 0-OH-DAG and showed extreme selectivity against nonhydroxylated DAG when competing substrates were present. 3. This selectivity explains the very low abundance of TAG species lacking ricinoleate in castor oil.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in yeast H1246 microsomes; in vitro enzyme assays with defined DAG/acyl-CoA substrates; DAG competition/selectivity assays; radiolabeling; TLC quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing seed/endosperm microsomal assays; substrate mixtures containing ricinoleoyl-CoA and nonhydroxylated acyl-CoAs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: enzyme is likely specific for sn-1,2-DAG over sn-2,3-DAG; can acylate nonhydroxylated DAG as single substrate, but such activity is strongly suppressed in competition assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: valuable for engineering unusual-fatty-acid oils; should be stacked with castor hydroxylase and complementary acyl-editing enzymes to maximize ricinoleate-rich TAG while minimizing nonhydroxylated TAG formation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: may channel flux away from common TAG species and create accumulation of nonhydroxylated DAG pools not used for TAG synthesis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL1 promoter (galactose-inducible) in yeast expression system
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial oil quality trait via ricinoleic acid enrichment
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: phosphatidylcholine/diacylglycerol interconversion linked to TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: PC-DAG exchange step in ER glycerolipid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reversibly exchanges phosphocholine between phosphatidylcholine and diacylglycerol, preferentially transferring 1-OH-DAG into PC and generating 0-OH-DAG from PC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for ricinoleate partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: active in yeast microsomes and developing castor endosperm microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes ricinoleate enrichment in TAG indirectly by selectively channeling 1-OH-DAG into PC while discriminating against 2-OH-DAG, thereby supporting formation of tri-ricinoleoyl TAG
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RcPDCT utilized ricinoleoyl-containing DAG efficiently, unlike Camelina PDCT. 2. RcPDCT showed over 10-fold selectivity for DAG with one ricinoleoyl group over DAG with two ricinoleoyl groups. 3. RcPDCT preferentially used 1-OH-DAG with ricinoleate at sn-2, producing PC species whose ricinoleate can be recycled or donated to TAG synthesis pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in yeast H1246 microsomes; [14C]G3P radiolabel assays; endogenous castor microsomal assays; positional analysis of DAG/PC species
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing endosperm; mixed ricinoleoyl-CoA and nonhydroxylated acyl-CoA substrate conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: although PDCT is an enzyme, in this study it has a flux-partitioning role beyond simple precursor return from PC to DAG; it exchanges mainly 1-OH-DAG for 0-OH-DAG and contributes to buildup of a nonhydroxylated DAG pool likely used in membrane lipid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: high-value engineering target to improve ricinoleate accumulation and restore oil content when combined with castor hydroxylase in transgenic oilseeds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: may increase accumulation of 0-OH-DAG pools not used for TAG synthesis
